creator: pgollucci@p6m7g8.com
text:
Trix Farrar wrote:
> If memory serves, devel/apr was installed back when it was supported
> under www/apache20.  At the point when it became unsupported, I
> believe that it should have thrown a warning or something.  I wasn't
> prepared to upgrade to apache22 yet, but did not realize (until this
> problem) that devel/apr was no longer supported for www/apache20.
I don't think the ports system as a way to account for that particular change.
Hopefully UPDATING had a relevant entry... Thats the best I can do for you at 
the moment though.

> You're right, upgrading to www/apache22 solved the problem (as I'm
> guessing uninstalling devel/apr might have).  But, if I hadn't been
> building packages for all my other systems, woldn't this error have
> continued to go unnoticed?
Possibly.

*technically* apr 0.9.x *can* be used by www/apache22; but nothing is 
officially tested with it or distributed.  The ports are just trying
to mirror 'reality' as best they can.

http://apr.apache.org/versioning.html
